response.justification: Candidate has ~14 years of solid full-stack and infrastructure engineering experience across diverse domains (web, mobile, embedded, GIS, high-throughput microservices) with demonstrated craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, workshops, OSS founder). However, the role is a Director-level IC Principal Engineer focused on AI strategy, requiring recognized AI expertise, demonstrated AI products/platforms, and the gravitas to advise strategic customers. The resume shows only recent, light exposure to AI-agent infrastructure (MCP tooling at re:cinq since April 2025) with no demonstrated AI/ML products, no research background, no thought leadership in AI, and no evidence of operating at a Principal/Director scope influencing org-wide strategy.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput microservices
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, OSS founder of GISCollective, workshops and talks
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators
concerns: No demonstrated AI/ML products, platforms, or recognized AI expertise required for the role
No evidence of Principal/Director-level scope, strategy influence, or advising strategic customers
Cloud experience is limited (AWS CDK, Kubernetes deploys) rather than deep cloud-platform strategy

key_factors.factor: Lack of recognized AI expertise / AI products built
